message:
fix: replace silent u128-to-u64 truncation across 24 files with saturating .min(u128::from(u64::MAX))

Systematic sweep replacing bare `.as_nanos() as u64` and
`.as_millis() as u64` casts with the saturating pattern
`.as_nanos().min(u128::from(u64::MAX)) as u64`.

The bare `as u64` silently truncates when the u128 value exceeds
u64::MAX (~584 years for nanos, ~584 million years for millis).
While these values are unreachable in practice, the truncation
is a correctness bug that violates the project's no-silent-data-loss
policy and could produce confusing results in property tests with
extreme durations.

Files affected (24): dns/cache.rs, dns/resolver.rs, tcp/listener.rs,
server/connection.rs, service/discover.rs, runtime/deadline_monitor.rs,
stream/debounce.rs, stream/throttle.rs, test_logging.rs,
tls/connector.rs (rustfmt only), conformance bench/runner.rs,
conformance logging.rs, conformance runner.rs, conformance
tests/{cancellation,negative,runtime}.rs, examples/{demo_benchmark,
demo_delta_debug,demo_record_nondeterministic}.rs,
tests/{common/e2e_harness,e2e_t6_data_path,network_fault_simulation,
pool_timeout_test,tokio_fs_process_signal_e2e}.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,7 +54,7 @@ impl ScenarioLog {
         self.entries.push(LogEntry {
             scenario_id: self.scenario_id,
             phase,
-            timestamp_ms: self.start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
+            timestamp_ms: self.start.elapsed().as_millis().min(u128::from(u64::MAX)) as u64,
             resource,
             action,
             outcome,
